details.description: Frontal view of a ruined Cham Hindu temple in a humid, forested valley. The structure is built of dark reddish-brown fired brick with weathered surfaces and missing upper portions. A steep, stepped platform of sandstone and brick leads to a rectangular doorway set within a tall, triangular corbelled gable typical of Cham architecture. Pilaster-like brick ribs, recessed panels, and traces of carvings are visible. The temple is surrounded by low collapsed walls and substructures on a stone and pebble forecourt. Lush green trees and misty, rolling hills rise behind the monument under an overcast sky, giving a moody, rain-soaked atmosphere.

details.description: The image shows ancient Hindu temple ruins constructed from dark reddish-brown brick, with a central doorway framed by heavy masonry and stepped platforms. The structure appears to be a Cham-style tower or temple façade, now partially ruined, with irregular brickwork and a rectangular entrance opening. Surrounding rubble and low walls sit on a pebble-strewn ground. In the background, dense jungle and rolling hills fade into mist under a cloudy sky, suggesting a tropical forested site. The overall scene is characteristic of My Son (Mỹ Sơn) in Quảng Nam, Vietnam, a historic Cham temple complex.

details.description: Weathered red-brick ruins of a Cham Hindu temple tower rise from a stepped platform in a lush, misty valley. The structure features a narrow stairway with carved stone treads leading to a tall rectangular doorway framed by corbelled brickwork and pilaster-like vertical bands. Much of the outer wall has collapsed, exposing layered brick courses and sandstone details, with scattered altars and base elements in front. Dense green forest and low mountains form the background under a cloudy sky, characteristic of the secluded setting of the My Son Sanctuary in central Vietnam.

details.description: A ruin of a dark-red brick Cham temple complex set in a lush forested hillside. The central structure features a stepped, pyramid-like tower with an arched doorway, flanked by lower brick walls and stone staircases, all weathered by time. The scene resembles the My Son Sanctuary ruins in Quảng Nam Province, Vietnam, with dense green mountains in the background.

details.description: A vertical photograph capturing the ancient red brick ruins of a Cham temple structure, likely within the My Son Sanctuary, set against a dramatic backdrop of misty, jungle-covered mountains. In the foreground, a pebbled ground leads to a set of restored brick steps ascending to a central, roofless structure. This building features a characteristic corbelled archway (false arch) entrance and intricate pilasters typical of Champa architecture. To the left, a fragmented, jagged section of a brick wall frames the scene. The bricks are reddish-brown, weathered by time and the humid climate. The atmosphere is moody and overcast, with low-hanging clouds obscuring the peaks of the lush green hills in the distance.

details.description: The image captures the ancient brick ruins of a Champa temple at the My Son Sanctuary. The central structure features a distinctive corbelled arch doorway and is constructed from dark reddish-brown bricks, a hallmark of Champa architecture. Stone steps lead up to a raised platform where the main sanctuary stands. The ruins are set against a backdrop of lush, verdant mountains shrouded in mist under an overcast sky. The foreground is composed of a path covered in small, rounded river stones. The overall atmosphere is one of historical solemnity and natural beauty, highlighting the weathered textures of the centuries-old masonry.

details.description: Ruined brick sanctuary tower set on a stepped platform with a central staircase leading to a tall, narrow doorway framed by corbelled brickwork. The structure shows heavy weathering and partial collapse, exposing dark-red fired bricks and some sandstone steps and thresholds. The temple stands in a forested valley with misty, tree‑covered hills behind, under an overcast sky. The massing, brick technique, and form are characteristic of Cham (Champa) Hindu temple architecture.

details.description: An ancient Khmer temple ruin built of dark red brick sits on a stepped platform. The central structure is a partially collapsed, pyramid-like shrine with a rectangular doorway opening into the sanctum. The masonry shows layered, vertical pilasters and remnants of a gabled upper section. Surrounding lower walls and staircases rise from a ground scattered with brick fragments. The site is set in a humid tropical forest with green trees and misty hills in the background under a gray, overcast sky.

details.description: A misty, overcast scene of an ancient brick temple ruin set in a lush, forested valley. The structure is built of dark reddish bricks with corbelled brickwork forming a tall, pointed doorway. Several tiers of steps lead up to the entrance, with a carved central riser and heavily eroded, collapsed flanks. Rubble and low platforms surround the main shrine, and dense green trees and layered hills rise behind it under low clouds. The setting suggests a Southeast Asian Hindu temple tower (kalan) in the jungle.

details.description: A partially ruined ancient temple constructed of dark red bricks sits within a dense tropical forest. The central structure resembles a small tower or shrine built with stepped brick courses, with a large rectangular doorway at its front and weathered staircases leading up to it. Surrounding walls and platforms are crumbled, with piles of bricks and stone blocks in the foreground. The scene is misty, with lush green trees and hazy hills in the background and a gray overcast sky above.

entities.name: Temple ruin
entities.notes: Ancient brick temple ruin; Southeast Asian architectural features; weathered by time; site resembles Cham/Khmer-inspired masonry.

details.description: A vertical photograph capturing the ancient red-brick ruins of a temple complex, likely a gate tower or gopura, set against a dramatic backdrop of mist-shrouded, jungle-covered mountains. The central structure features a corbelled archway entrance and a flight of stone steps leading up to a dark interior, characteristic of Cham architecture. The brickwork is weathered and dark with age, showing intricate layering without visible mortar. To the left, a jagged, ruined wall section frames the scene. The foreground consists of a bed of smooth river stones or gravel. The atmosphere is moody and overcast, with low clouds obscuring the peaks of the lush green hills in the distance.

details.description: A photograph of the ancient red brick ruins of a Champa temple at the Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary in Vietnam. The central structure features a corbelled arch doorway and is partially collapsed, showing the intricate brickwork characteristic of Champa architecture. The ruins are set against a backdrop of lush, mist-covered green mountains under an overcast sky. The foreground consists of a dark, pebble-covered ground and another ruined wall section on the left.
